writeReadback.rule: The returned subject, role, surfaceAccess, permissions, tenantId, sessionId, phoneE164, and expiry replace every cached projection.

writeReadback.readback: GET /auth/session with the new access token
writeReadback.rule: The previous refresh token is invalid and must be removed from secure storage before the new pair is committed.
The revoked session disappears; revoking the current session clears local secure storage immediately.

writeReadback.readback: all prior access and refresh tokens fail
writeReadback.rule: No surface may continue from a locally cached authenticated state.

writeReadback.readback: session endpoints reject the old token and the deletion outbox contains the stable event
writeReadback.rule: The local session is cleared only after the server confirms deletion.

crossSurfaceInvariants: One phone maps to one sovereign actor even when multiple roles are attached.
Each surface checks both requiredRole and requiredSurface before rendering protected content.
A role added by Workforce is visible after session refresh; clients never append roles locally.
Deactivation and account deletion take effect on every surface through server token validation.
No runtime uses demo identity, hard-coded email, fabricated session, local actor table, or fixture as operational truth.
storageRule: Only the encrypted/platform secure session adapter may persist tokens; web and native runtimes use the same StoredSession envelope and server readback.
